description:
## Goal
`fixtures/quicksort/` as the closing fixture for the epic. Quicksort surfaced the need to destructure; this fixture proves the arc landed.

## Depends on
- E6 (parity gates already in place).

## Fixture content
Standard `fixtures/quicksort/` layout (mirrors `fib_tailrec/`): `input.fz`, `README.md`, `expected.txt`, `expected.clif`, `expected.specs`, `expected.outcomes`.

```
fn append([], ys), do: ys
fn append([h | t], ys), do: [h | append(t, ys)]

fn partition(_, [], lo, hi), do: {lo, hi}
fn partition(p, [h | t], lo, hi) do
  if h < p do partition(p, t, [h | lo], hi)
  else partition(p, t, lo, [h | hi]) end
end

fn qsort([]), do: []
fn qsort([p | rest]) do
  {lo, hi} = partition(p, rest, [], [])
  append(qsort(lo), [p | qsort(hi)])
end

fn main() do
  print(qsort([3, 1, 4, 1, 5, 9, 2, 6, 5, 3, 5]))
end
```

## Acceptance — three windows on the same arc
- **Functional**: output is sorted; all four legs agree.
- **CLIF**: `expected.clif` shows projection-only lowering at the `{lo, hi} = partition(...)` site — no synthesized `match_error` halt, no dead block. Visible proof E4 (`ir_branch_fold`) did its job on a real recursive program.
- **Specs / outcomes**: `expected.specs` records the polymorphic specs `qsort` and `partition` resolve to; `expected.outcomes` records the callsite outcomes published by the typer.

## Note on `append/2`
Not tail-recursive (`[h | append(t, ys)]`). Fine for an 11-element demo. The fixture is feature coverage, not a performance benchmark. README states this.

## Done when
- Fixture exists with all required snapshots.
- All four legs pass.
- `expected.clif` visibly demonstrates branch-fold cleanup at the destructure site (no `match_error` halt block, no `Halt(:match_error)` term anywhere in qsort/partition CLIF).
title: E7: fixtures/quicksort/ — closing fixture, expected.clif proves the arc
